issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline to none. While it defines an outline-offset, it fails to provide a visible replacement (like a box-shadow or border) within this specific rule, potentially leaving elements without a visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 318: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the outline for elements focused via non-keyboard methods. If a browser fails to correctly apply the :focus-visible polyfill or logic, interactive elements will have no focus indicator at all.
issuesFound.fix: Line 325: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; } /* Ensure :focus-visible is robustly defined above */

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component explicitly removes the outline on focus-visible. Although it has transitions for background-color, it does not provide a high-contrast focus ring, which is required for accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 614: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link removes the outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative style like a border or box-shadow.
issuesFound.fix: Line 718: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the focus outline, which prevents keyboard users from identifying their location when navigating the header.
issuesFound.fix: Line 751: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the outline on focus. While they have a border-radius, they lack a visible high-contrast indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 818: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the default outline. While it adds a box-shadow, box-shadow often disappears in Windows High Contrast Mode (Forced Colors), leaving the element without a focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 851: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly removes the outline for focus-visible, which is the primary mechanism for keyboard focus visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 857: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submission button removes the focus outline without a visible alternative, blocking keyboard interaction clarity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 878: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Links within the account navigation remove the focus outline, making it difficult for keyboard users to navigate the utility menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 904: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the focus outline. Since these links often use a pseudo-element to cover the whole card, the lack of a focus ring makes the entire card's focus state invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1114: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Product filter buttons remove the focus outline, which is a failure for users navigating the filter interface via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1264: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle buttons remove the focus outline, hindering the experience for keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1444: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table container is focusable (tabindex="0") but removes its focus indicator, meaning keyboard users won't know when they have focused the region to scroll it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1574: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion triggers remove the focus outline, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which question they are about to expand.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1704: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the default outline. The replacement box-shadow is insufficient for users in high-contrast modes where shadows are stripped.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1754: .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form elements explicitly remove the focus-visible outline, failing to provide a standard-compliant focus appearance.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1755: .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 AA requirement of 24x24px for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 684: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons are set to a min-width and min-height of 20px, failing the 24x24px minimum target size requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 698: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu toggle has a min-height and min-width of 28px. While it meets the 24px absolute minimum, it fails the 44px recommended target size for better pointer ergonomics.
issuesFound.fix: Line 740: .nav-toggle { display: none; min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links have a min-height of 28px, which is significantly below the 44px recommendation for interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 774: #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { ... min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links have a min-height of 28px, making them difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 806: [id$="-submenu"] li a { ... min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input height is set to 28px, which is a small target for touch and pointer users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 835: #search-input { width: 220px; height: 44px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28x28px, which is below the 44px target size recommendation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 865: #site-search button[type="submit"] { ... width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .product-body .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: The secondary button within product cards has a min-height of 28px, which is a small target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1338: .product-body .btn-secondary { ... min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons are 28px tall, which is below the recommended 44px for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1422: .pricing-toggle button { ... min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses "position: static", "width: auto", and "clip: auto". This implementation fails to visually hide the content while keeping it in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 354: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition (translateY) to slide into view, but there is no override to disable this motion for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 404: .skip-link { ... transition: transform var(--transition-base); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: translateY(0); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes a transition for 'transform', which is used for the active state (translateY). This motion is not disabled for reduced motion preferences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 608: .btn { ... transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses a complex 3D transform transition (perspective, rotateY, rotateX) that triggers on hover. This significant spatial movement lacks a reduced motion guard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1018: .hero-image img { ... transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards use a transform transition (translateY) on hover. While a media query exists at line 1100, it only targets the hover state and not the base transition property.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1088: .feature-card { ... transition: ... transform var(--transition-base); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards use a transform transition (translateY) on hover without a global reduced motion override for the transition property itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1288: .product-card { ... transition: ... transform var(--transition-base); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: Stat items use a transform transition (translateY) on hover. The media query at line 1390 only disables the hover transform but not the transition itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1378: .stat-item { ... transition: ... transform var(--transition-base); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards use a transform transition (translateY) on hover without a proper reduced motion guard for the transition property.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1478: .pricing-card { ... transition: ... transform var(--transition-base); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em. Negative word-spacing pushes words closer together, significantly impairing readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 218: body { ... word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing to -0.05em. Tightening the space between characters reduces legibility and violates text spacing best practices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 219: body { ... letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6
issuesFound.explanation: Headings use a negative letter-spacing variable (--letter-spacing-tight), which can make characters overlap or become indistinguishable for some users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 230: h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { ... letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero H1 uses --letter-spacing-tighter (-0.05em), which creates extremely tight character spacing that hinders reading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 964: .hero-content h1 { ... letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .stat-value
issuesFound.explanation: Stat values use --letter-spacing-tighter, which can make large numbers difficult to read clearly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1400: .stat-value { ... letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.3:1
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary variable is set to #aaaaaa. When used on the default white background (#ffffff), the contrast ratio is 2.32:1, which is well below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 104: --colour-text-secondary: #6b6b6b;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #ee9999 / background #ffffff ratio 2.4:1
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-error variable is set to #ee9999. On a white background, this provides a contrast ratio of 2.45:1, making error messages or markers unreadable for many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 92: --colour-error: #d32f2f;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global 'a' selector removes text-decoration. In the .prose sections or body text, links will only be distinguishable by colour, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with colour blindness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 246: a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Readability concern: font-size below 16px on html
issuesFound.explanation: The root font-size is set to 12px. This makes the base 'rem' unit very small, leading to body text that is difficult to read without significant zooming.
issuesFound.fix: Line 200: html { ... font-size: 16px; }

issuesFound.title: Readability concern: line-height below 1.5 on p
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraphs are set to a line-height of 1. This is extremely cramped and violates the WCAG AAA recommendation of 1.5 for body text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 238: p { line-height: 1.5; text-wrap: pretty; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero H1 uses --letter-spacing-tighter (-0.05em), which creates extremely tight character spacing that hinders reading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 964: .hero-content h1 { ... letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .stat-value
issuesFound.explanation: Stat values use --letter-spacing-tighter, which can make large numbers difficult to read clearly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1400: .stat-value { ... letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.3:1
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary variable is set to #aaaaaa. When used on the default white background (#ffffff), the contrast ratio is 2.32:1, which is well below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 104: --colour-text-secondary: #6b6b6b;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #ee9999 / background #ffffff ratio 2.4:1
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-error variable is set to #ee9999. On a white background, this provides a contrast ratio of 2.45:1, making error messages or markers unreadable for many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 92: --colour-error: #d32f2f;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global 'a' selector removes text-decoration. In the .prose sections or body text, links will only be distinguishable by colour, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with colour blindness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 246: a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Readability concern: font-size below 16px on html
issuesFound.explanation: The root font-size is set to 12px. This makes the base 'rem' unit very small, leading to body text that is difficult to read without significant zooming.
issuesFound.fix: Line 200: html { ... font-size: 16px; }

issuesFound.title: Readability concern: line-height below 1.5 on p

issuesFound.fix: Line 238: p { line-height: 1.5; text-wrap: pretty; }

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class and body scroll lock but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation menu has been deployed or retracted.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1445: `open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }`
       Line 1459: `close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function changes the layout between 'grid' and 'list' modes and updates a CSS class, but it does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the button that was just clicked. Sighted users see the change, but assistive technology users are not informed which toggle state is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2415: `function _onViewToggle(e) { ... const mode = btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode'); if (!mode || mode === _productFilterState.viewMode) return; _productFilterState.viewMode = mode; btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true'); qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { if (b !== btn) b.setAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false'); }); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product filter is applied, the `_updateCountBadge` function updates the text content of the count badge, but there is no call to a live region to announce the new number of results. Users of assistive technology will not know how many products remain after a filter is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2358: `function _updateCountBadge(count) { if (!_countBadge) return; _countBadge.textContent = String(count); setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', `${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`); liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${count} products`); }`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module updates the error message text and toggles visibility, but it does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field itself. Screen readers will not identify the field as being in an error state when the user navigates back to it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3118: `function _renderResult(field, result, errorEl) { if (result.valid) { field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); ... } else { field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); ... } }`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `clearField` function resets the error message and CSS classes but fails to reset or remove the `aria-invalid` attribute. A field that was previously invalid will continue to be announced as invalid even after being cleared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3181: `function clearField(field) { ... removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); removeClass(field, CSS.fieldValid); field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); }`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load

issuesFound.fix: Line 1515: `function init() { ... _initialised = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility .sr-only

issuesFound.explanation: .btn:active has transform: translateY(1px) with a transition, causing spatial movement without a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override. This can trigger discomfort or nausea for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. This can make buttons difficult to tap for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line where .btn-sm is defined: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-height: 20px and min-width: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons become hard to activate, especially on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line where .btn-icon is defined: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-3); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and overflow: visible, which does not hide content visually and may expose it to screen readers incorrectly, breaking accessible hiding techniques.
issuesFound.fix: Line where .sr-only is defined: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform and transition animations without a reduced-motion override, which can cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Add within the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability, particularly for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line where body is defined: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-primary uses background-color: #5588bb and color: #ffffff, with a contrast ratio of approximately 3:1, below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. This makes text hard to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line where .btn-primary is defined: .btn-primary { background-color: #005fcc; border-color: #005fcc; color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-secondary uses background-color: #ffffff and color: #555555, with a contrast ratio of about 4.2:1, below the 4.5:1 threshold. This reduces text legibility for users with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line where .btn-secondary is defined: .btn-secondary { background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-border-strong); color: #333333; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines without providing an alternative visual cue, relying solely on colour to distinguish links. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with colour blindness.
issuesFound.fix: Line where a is defined: a { text-decoration: underline; color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation menu toggle does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button when the menu is opened or closed. Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded state of the menu, which is a critical piece of information for understanding the current interface state.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.open function, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); In the mobileNav.close function, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu toggle does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button when the dropdown is opened or closed. Screen reader users will not know whether the dropdown is expanded or collapsed, breaking accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu._openInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); In dropdownMenu._closeInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion toggle does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button when the answer is expanded or collapsed. Screen reader users will not be able to determine the expanded state of each FAQ item.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open, add: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); In faqAccordion.close, add: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: billing period toggle does not update aria-pressed on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons do not have aria-pressed set to indicate which button is active. Screen reader users will not know which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing._syncToggleState, for each button, set aria-pressed to 'true' for the active button and 'false' for the others.

issuesFound.title: productFilter does not announce filter results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the updated product count and visible cards are not announced to screen reader users. This makes it difficult for users who rely on screen readers to understand the results of their filtering actions.
issuesFound.fix: In productFilter.applyFilters, after updating the count and empty state, add a live region announcement, e.g., liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${totalMatches} products`);

issuesFound.title: pricing module does not announce billing period change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is changed (monthly/annual), there is no announcement to screen reader users. This means users who rely on screen readers may not be aware of the change in pricing.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing.setPeriod, after updating the prices, add a live region announcement, e.g., liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid to false when field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field that was previously invalid becomes valid, the aria-invalid attribute is not set to 'false'. This can cause screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid, even though it is now valid.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult, when the field is valid, set setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); Alternatively, in srUtils.markValid, set setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an initial aria-expanded state set when the page loads. This means screen reader users will not know the initial state of the menu (which is closed).
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, after getting the trigger and drawer, set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open(), mobileNav.close(), and mobileNav.toggle() functions change the visual state of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen-reader users will not be informed of the menu's state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1378: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
Line 1398: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`
Line 1407: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, _open);`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the associated trigger button. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern for screen-reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1557: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
Line 1573: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions expand/collapse FAQ answer panels but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the question button (item.trigger). Screen-reader users cannot track the accordion's state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2988: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
Line 3008: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: Product filter application does not announce results to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible card count and filters the grid but does not announce the change via an ARIA live region. Screen-reader users are not informed when filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2295: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing ${totalMatches} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates all plan card prices and the UI but does not provide a live-region announcement. Screen-reader users will not hear that the billing period has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2634: add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors do not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function adds error classes and messages but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field element when validation fails. Screen-reader users may not be alerted to the field's invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3448: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, !result.valid);`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on mobile nav trigger at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set an initial aria-expanded state on the navigation toggle button (_trigger). When the page loads with the menu closed, the button lacks the required aria-expanded="false" attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1531: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on dropdown triggers at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets up dropdown triggers but does not assign an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute when the panel is hidden. Screen readers need the initial state to be declared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1617: add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

fpTitles: aria-expanded not initialised on dropdown triggers at page load

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open, mobileNav.close, and mobileNav.toggle functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users and keyboard users relying on assistive technology will not be informed of the menu's open/closed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and dropdownMenu._closeInstance functions show/hide dropdown panels without setting aria-expanded on their trigger buttons. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern, leaving screen reader users unaware of the dropdown's state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2100: In _openInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
Line 2101: In _closeInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function updates visual active states on billing period buttons but does not set aria-pressed. Users of assistive technology cannot perceive which billing period is selected, failing WCAG 4.1.2 for toggle button states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: Inside the forEach loop, add: setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', isActive ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter results update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product count and grid but does not announce these changes via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filter results change, degrading the experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2300: After _updateCountBadge(totalMatches), add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors do not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields as invalid visually and with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input elements. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state, violating WCAG 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3400: In the else block (invalid case), add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');
Line 3401: In the valid case, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button not initialised with aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is closed on page load, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "false" initially. Screen reader users may not know the button controls a collapsible element until after first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1995: In the mobileNav.init function, after _trigger is assigned, add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons not initialised with aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus are closed on page load, but their trigger buttons lack an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute. This omission prevents screen readers from understanding the widget's initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2095: In the dropdownMenu.register function, after setting aria-haspopup and aria-controls, add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name

'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}>

issuesFound.explanation: The “.nav-toggle” button that opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer never receives an aria‑expanded attribute on page load. Assistive technologies cannot know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 2.1 ARIA state requirements.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu triggers do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register` the trigger button is given `aria-haspopup` but never receives an aria‑expanded attribute when the panel is opened or closed. Screen readers cannot perceive the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1320: after `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-haspopup', 'true');` also add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.  
       Line 1335 (inside `_openInstance`): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.  
       Line 1345 (inside `_closeInstance`): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ items’ trigger buttons are given `aria-controls` and `role="button"` but the script never toggles `aria‑expanded` when an item is opened or closed, so AT users cannot tell which answer is visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2102 (in `faqRegistry.parse`): after `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.  
       Line 2120 (in `faqAccordion.open`): add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.  
       Line 2130 (in `faqAccordion.close`): add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (`[data-period]`) are toggled with CSS classes but never receive an aria‑pressed attribute, leaving the current period ambiguous to assistive tech.

Line 2505 (in `pricing._syncToggleState`): after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.  
       Line 2518 (initialisation): after creating the buttons, set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` for all, then rely on `_syncToggleState` to update.

issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing toggle (`[data-billing-toggle]`) is given `role="switch"` but never receives an aria‑pressed value when toggled, so screen readers cannot know its state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2602 (in `pricing._syncToggleState` after `toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear);`): add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear));`.  
       Line 2615 (initialisation): set `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `srUtils.markInvalid` only adds `aria-describedby` but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the offending input. Likewise `srUtils.markValid` never clears `aria-invalid`. This violates WCAG 2.1 ARIA error indication.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 822 (in `srUtils.markInvalid`): after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`.  
       Line 1510 (in `inlineValidator.validate` where `srUtils.markInvalid` is called): ensure that `srUtils.markValid` (line 825) also calls `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge` changes the numeric text of the product count badge but does not send the change to a live region, so users of assistive technology are unaware of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1758 (inside `_updateCountBadge`): after setting `textContent`, also call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count!==1?'s':''} found`);` or update an existing `aria-live` element.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce price updates
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing._updatePlanCard` updates the displayed price and period label without notifying a live region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new pricing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2423 (after updating `plan.priceDisplay.textContent`): add `liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${plan.priceDisplay.textContent} ${plan.pricePeriod.textContent}`);` or update a dedicated `aria-live` element for pricing announcements.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `toggle()` function opens or closes the mobile drawer but never changes the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). AT therefore cannot know whether the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(!_open));` (or update inside `open()`/`close()`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance(inst)` adds CSS classes to show the panel but does not set `aria‑expanded` on the button that controls it, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_closeInstance` set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded on question button
issuesFound.explanation: `open(item, …)` expands the answer panel but never toggles `aria‑expanded` on the question button, so screen readers cannot detect the open state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening and set to `'false'` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: `setPeriod()` visually marks the active period button with a CSS class but never updates `aria‑pressed`, leaving assistive tech unaware of which period is active.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState(period)` iterate over `_periodBtns` and call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btnPeriod===period));`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is created without an `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute, so its collapsed default state is not conveyed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init()` after locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered, the trigger button gets `aria-haspopup` but no `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its collapsed state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: In `register(trigger, panel)` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ question button missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are parsed and the trigger gets `aria-controls` but no default `aria-expanded="false"`, so AT cannot know they start collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqRegistry.parse()` after obtaining `trigger`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` marks fields with error messages but never toggles the `aria‑invalid` attribute, so screen readers cannot detect invalid inputs.
issuesFound.fix: After a failed validation, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and on success remove or set to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter updates count badge without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the product count badge (`_countBadge`) changes text, but no ARIA live region is updated, leaving users unaware of how many results are shown.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden element `
` and in `_updateCountBadge(count)` set its `textContent` to `"${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} shown"`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change does not announce to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: `setPeriod()` updates UI but does not inform AT of the new period, so users miss the change.
issuesFound.fix: After UI updates, call `liveRegion.announce(\`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label.toLowerCase()}\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module opens and closes the off‑canvas drawer, but the .nav‑toggle button never receives an aria‑expanded attribute reflecting the drawer’s state. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In the open() function set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in close() set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav‑toggle button controls the navigation drawer but does not have an aria‑controls attribute pointing to the drawer’s id, violating the requirement that toggle controls reference the element they show/hide.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id); after the drawer element is identified.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu buttons lack initial aria‑expanded="false" on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger buttons are given aria‑haspopup but no initial aria‑expanded value, so screen readers assume an unknown state before interaction.
issuesFound.fix: In register() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); when the trigger is first processed.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed when switched
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing toggle ( [data‑billing‑toggle] ) changes visual state but never updates aria‑pressed, leaving AT users unaware of the current period.
issuesFound.fix: In _ onToggleClick() after toggling the period call setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false'); and set the same attribute on initialisation.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult adds error messages and CSS classes but never sets aria‑invalid on the field, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult when !result.valid add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); and when result.valid remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not clear aria‑invalid when the field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: The same _renderResult function removes error text on valid input but does not clear aria‑invalid, leaving the field permanently marked as invalid for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In the else branch of _renderResult call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');.

issuesFound.title: Product count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter._updateCountBadge updates the numeric badge but does not use a live region, so users of assistive technology are unaware of the number of matching products after filtering.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> inside the badge element and update its textContent with the new count each time the badge changes.

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle does not announce the change via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between grid and list view updates CSS classes but provides no audible feedback; AT users cannot know which view is active.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the view, update a dedicated live region (e.g., ) with “View switched to list view” or “View switched to grid view”.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce the new period to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod updates button states and price text but does not use an ARIA live region, so users are not informed when the billing period switches (e.g., from monthly to annual).
issuesFound.fix: After setting the period, set textContent of a hidden live region (e.g., ) to “Billing period set to annual”.

fpTitles: Inline form validation does not clear aria‑invalid when the field becomes valid

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a navigation submenu (NavDropdown) controls visibility with state isOpen but does not expose its state via aria‑expanded, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of whether the submenu is open.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens the off‑canvas mobile navigation panel toggles the panel’s visibility but does not convey its state with aria‑expanded, so assistive technologies cannot tell if the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without defining a visible alternative such as a border or box-shadow. This removes the focus indicator for all interactive elements relying on this rule, making keyboard navigation impossible to track for users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector explicitly removes the outline but does not provide a visible replacement like a box-shadow or border. Keyboard users will not see which button is focused, violating WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Visually hidden utility class renders content visibly
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide elements visually. Content intended for screen readers only will be visible on the page, cluttering the interface and confusing users.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector applies word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words. This can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision, violating WCAG 1.4.12.
issuesFound.fix: Line 157: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section text
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.4:1, which fails the WCAG 1.4.3 minimum requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 726: color: var(--colour-neutral-700);

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for icon button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24px, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for checkbox
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width and height to 20px. This fails the WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24px, creating a barrier for touch and pointer users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes a transition on transform properties without a corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience disorientation from this animation.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links remove text-decoration and rely solely on colour change on hover to indicate interactivity. Users with colour vision deficiencies may not perceive these elements as links, violating WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without defining a visible alternative like border or box-shadow. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all interactive elements globally, failing WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector explicitly removes the outline without providing a visible replacement. Even if a global style exists, this specific rule overrides it, hiding focus state for button components.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to visually hide content. This exposes screen-reader-only text to sighted users, cluttering the interface and failing WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform transitions that cause spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion, failing WCAG 2.3.3.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which condenses text and reduces legibility for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision. This contradicts WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing recommendations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 253: word-spacing: 0em;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary selector uses #5588bb text on a #ffffff background (via inheritance) or vice versa. The contrast ratio is approximately 3.38:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text under WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum).
issuesFound.fix: Line 490: color: #003888;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section header paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses #aaaaaa text on a #ffffff background. The contrast ratio is 2.85:1, which is below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text, making it difficult for low-vision users to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 729: color: var(--colour-neutral-700);

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width and min-height to 20px. This is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum), making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a selector removes text-decoration without providing a non-colour differentiator at rest (underline appears only on hover). Relying on colour alone to distinguish links fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally on keyboard focus
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow. This removes the focus ring for all keyboard users across the site, making navigation impossible for those who rely on visual focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on button components
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule explicitly removes the outline without defining a replacement border or box-shadow. Keyboard users tabbing to buttons will see no visual indication of which element is active.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. This critical interactive element becomes invisible to keyboard users when focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible rule removes the outline without adding a border or box-shadow. Links within feature cards will not show focus state to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on filter tab buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Users navigating filters via keyboard cannot identify the selected tab.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible rule removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see which billing period is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on FAQ accordion buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a border or box-shadow. Keyboard users cannot identify which FAQ question is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible rule removes the outline without a visible alternative. Footer links become inaccessible to keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on social media links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a replacement. Social media icons/links will not show focus state to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible rule removes the outline without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see which checkbox is focused.

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px (SC 2.5.8). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons require larger targets for touch accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly sized to 20px by 20px, which is below the 24px minimum target size. This makes toggling the checkbox difficult for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Screen reader utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which means content is NOT hidden visually. This exposes screen-reader-only text to all users, cluttering the interface.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element has a transform transition defined, but the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 933 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class defines transitions for background, border, color, box-shadow, and transform. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disabling these transitions for buttons.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word spacing can cause letters to overlap or words to become illegible for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for table status text
issuesFound.explanation: The text color var(--colour-neutral-300) (#cbd5e1) on a white background (#ffffff) has a contrast ratio of approximately 1.3:1, which is far below the required 4.5:1. Users with low vision cannot read this status text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without consistent alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. While some links have hover underlines, many (e.g., footer links) rely solely on color change to indicate interactivity, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

fpTitles: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for buttons

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons toggle the panel visibility but do not update aria-expanded. The register function also fails to initialize aria-expanded="false" on new instances. Assistive technology users are not informed when a dropdown opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open function sets the panel to visible but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state where screen readers may announce the answer as collapsed even when it is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2735: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox input does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but its aria-expanded attribute is never updated when the suggestion list opens or closes. Screen reader users are not informed that the listbox is available or when it is dismissed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2495: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
Line 2510: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox input aria-expanded not initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer mount function configures the combobox input but does not initialize aria-expanded="false". The attribute should reflect the closed state on page load before any interaction occurs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2345: setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle element has role="switch" but the _syncToggleState function only toggles CSS classes. It does not update aria-checked to reflect whether monthly or annual billing is active, preventing screen reader users from knowing the current setting.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3340: setAttr(btn, ARIA.CHECKED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs do not update aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons manage the active category but _syncTabAriaStates only toggles the CSS active class. It does not update aria-selected (or aria-pressed) to indicate which filter is currently applied to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3040: setAttr(btn, ARIA.SELECTED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error description but does not remove or set aria-invalid="false". The field may remain in an invalid state programmatically even after the user corrects the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1405: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle CSS classes and visibility but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the navigation trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1645: function open(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }
  Line 1675: function close(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu instance functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions manage the panel visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technology from conveying the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2045: function _openInstance(inst) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }
  Line 2065: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function fails to set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() function sets the panel to visible but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While close() correctly sets it to false, the open state is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3345: function open(item, announceToSR) { ... setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid without this attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1275: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { ... field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); }

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState does not update aria-selected on period buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function updates the visual active class on billing period buttons but does not update the aria-selected attribute. Users relying on assistive technology will not know which period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2685: function _syncToggleState(period) { ... setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive)); ... }

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop._onClick does not announce action to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button click handler scrolls the page and moves focus but does not announce the action via a live region. Users may be disoriented by the sudden viewport change without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2235: function _onClick() { ... liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page'); }

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner does not update trigger aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the cookie consent banner is shown (either on load or via preferences button), the code does not update the triggering button's aria-expanded attribute to reflect the open state of the dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3980: function _showBanner() { ... const trigger = qs('[data-cookie-prefs]'); if(trigger) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce count changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the product count badge text and aria-label but does not push an announcement to a live region. Screen reader users will not be notified that the number of results has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2445: function applyFilters() { ... liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button (_trigger) controls the visibility of the drawer, but the open() and close() functions modify classes and scroll state without updating the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger. Screen reader users will not be informed when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Function open(): setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line Function close(): setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger buttons (inst.trigger) control the panel visibility, but _openInstance and _closeInstance only toggle the hidden attribute and CSS classes. The aria-expanded state on the trigger is never updated, breaking the disclosure pattern for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Function _openInstance(): setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line Function _closeInstance(): setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger (item.trigger). The close() function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state where screen readers are not notified of expansion.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function open(): setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (_periodBtns) act as a radio group or toggle set, but _syncToggleState only updates CSS classes. The aria-pressed attribute is not updated to reflect which period is currently active, leaving screen reader users unaware of the selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function _syncToggleState(): setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as invalid, markInvalid() links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to announce the error state immediately upon focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function markInvalid(): setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is validated as correct, markValid() removes the error description but does not remove or reset the aria-invalid attribute. If the field was previously invalid, aria-invalid="true" may persist, causing screen readers to incorrectly announce an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function markValid(): field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge (_countBadge) text content is updated dynamically when filters are applied, but the element lacks an aria-live attribute. Screen reader users are not notified that the number of products has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function init(): setAttr(_countBadge, ARIA.LIVE, 'polite');

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The savings message element (_savingsMsg) is updated with new text when the billing period changes, but it does not have an aria-live region. Users relying on assistive technology will not hear the updated savings information.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function init(): setAttr(_savingsMsg, ARIA.LIVE, 'polite');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded is not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger (_trigger) is created or selected on load, but its initial aria-expanded state is not set to "false". Assistive technologies may not correctly report the initial closed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function init(): setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger aria-expanded is not initialised on registration
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown pair is registered, the panel is hidden, but the trigger's aria-expanded attribute is not initialised to "false". This leaves the initial state ambiguous for screen readers until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function register(): setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button aria-pressed is not initialised if autoplay is off
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button (_playBtn) manages the carousel rotation state. If autoplay is disabled or reduced motion is preferred, the play() function is not called during init, leaving aria-pressed uninitialised. Screen readers won't know the initial playback state.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function init(): setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating the page will not see any focus indicator on interactive elements, making it impossible to track their current position.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector only adjusts outline-offset but inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule. No visible focus indicator is provided, preventing keyboard users from seeing when elements with this class receive focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 352: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width/height: auto, which makes the content visible instead of hiding it visually. Additionally, it lacks absolute positioning and clipping, meaning content is not properly hidden from visual view while remaining accessible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360-371: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transform transition and hover animation that causes spatial movement. The prefers-reduced-motion media query at line 640 is empty and does not disable this animation, which may cause vestibular disorders for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 640-643: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to overlap in some fonts or zoom levels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 324: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The body sets color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa against a white background (#ffffff). This results in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements of 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision will have difficulty reading secondary text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 24: --colour-text-secondary: #767676; (or darker to achieve 4.5:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer navigation

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on social links
