issuesFound.title: Screen reader utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only selector uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide the content. Content intended only for screen readers will be visible to all users, breaking the layout and potentially exposing hidden text.

issuesFound.title: Button motion without reduced-motion support
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition properties, causing spatial movement that cannot be disabled by users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Hero image motion without reduced-motion support
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transform in transition, causing 3D perspective rotation effects that cannot be disabled.

issuesFound.title: Feature card motion without reduced-motion support
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card selector includes transform: translateY in its hover transition, causing spatial movement that cannot be disabled.

issuesFound.title: Pricing card motion without reduced-motion support
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card selector includes transform in its transition, causing spatial movement that cannot be disabled.

issuesFound.title: FAQ button icon motion without reduced-motion support
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button::after selector includes transform rotation in its transition, causing motion that cannot be disabled.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces the space between words and characters. This can make text harder to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause text to overlap at certain zoom levels.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~135: Remove word-spacing: -0.2em; and letter-spacing: -0.05em; or set to normal.

issuesFound.title: Secondary text colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa. On the white background (var(--colour-bg-page) #ffffff), this provides a contrast ratio of approximately 2.2:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~525: Change color to var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Blockquote text colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) which has insufficient contrast (2.2:1) against the white background.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~255: Change color to var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Stats label colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label selector uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% white) against a gradient background. Against the lighter part of the gradient (#bb88ee), this provides insufficient contrast for text.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1140: Change to rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9);

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed globally without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none globally without providing an alternative visual differentiator such as border-bottom or font-weight. Links are only distinguishable by color, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive color differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~165: Add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; or remove text-decoration: none and rely on browser defaults.

issuesFound.title: Base font size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size: 12px, making the base rem unit 12px. With body font-size set to 1rem (12px), this is below the 16px recommended minimum for body text, potentially causing readability issues for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~122: font-size: 100%; (respects user browser settings) or font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class does not hide content from visual view
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which keeps the content visible to all users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks layouts and creates visual clutter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 383-394: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link has transition: transform var(--transition-base) which may cause motion sensitivity issues, and there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 402: (add to existing @media block at line 415) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle has transition properties for background-color and border-color. While less severe than transform, it should still respect reduced-motion preferences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 461: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .nav-toggle { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing and letter-spacing impair readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces text spacing and can make content harder to read for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 263-264: word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) on a background of rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08) from .stat-item, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.2:1, far below the required 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on pricing toggle badge
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button[aria-pressed="true"] .badge uses color: var(--colour-neutral-0) (#ffffff) on background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.25) over the brand primary color (#5588bb), resulting in insufficient contrast (approximately 2.1:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 970: background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); color: var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter paragraph text
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section > p uses color: rgba(255,255,255,0.8) on a gradient background containing #5588bb, resulting in insufficient contrast for the 12px font size (below 4.5:1 requirement).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1173: color: #ffffff;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input hints
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa on a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of 2.3:1, below the required 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1198: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter input hints
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section .input-hint uses color: rgba(255,255,255,0.65) on the gradient background, resulting in insufficient contrast.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed globally without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Without a global alternative visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or background on hover), links rely solely on color to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 281: a { text-decoration: underline; } OR add a:hover { text-decoration: underline; } and ensure links have sufficient color contrast

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed on footer navigation without hover alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector removes text-decoration and the hover state only changes color without restoring the underline or adding another non-color visual indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1233: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { text-decoration: underline; color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in the dropdownMenu module show and hide dropdown panels but fail to set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger buttons. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded state of the dropdowns to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2050: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside _openInstance after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`.
  Line 2070: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside _closeInstance before `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close'...`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _open() and _close() functions in the siteSearch module manage the suggestion listbox visibility for the combobox pattern but do not toggle aria-expanded on the search input element. Screen readers cannot convey whether the suggestion list is currently displayed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2480: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` at the end of the _open() function.
  Line 2490: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the end of the _close() function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function activates and deactivates filter tab buttons based on the selected category but only toggles CSS classes. It does not set aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state, so screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850: Inside the forEach loop in _syncTabAriaStates, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function updates the visual state of billing period buttons (monthly/yearly) but only toggles CSS classes. It fails to set aria-pressed on the active button, preventing screen readers from announcing which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: Inside the forEach loop in _syncToggleState, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function in srUtils adds error descriptions to fields via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot identify that the field contains an invalid value, reducing the effectiveness of form validation feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1350: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` at the beginning of the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes error descriptions from fields but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute. Fields remain marked as invalid in the accessibility tree even after passing validation, causing confusion for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1360: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` at the beginning of the markValid function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates the product count badge and emits a custom event when filters are applied, but does not announce the filter results via a live region. Screen reader users receive no confirmation that filtering occurred or how many results are showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2750: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(s.activeCategory === (CONFIG.products.allCategoryValue || 'all') ? STRINGS.filterAll : STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function updates price displays and emits an event when the billing period changes, but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that prices have switched between monthly and annual billing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100: After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop click handler missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _onClick() function in scrollToTop smoothly scrolls the page to the top but does not announce the action completion. Screen reader users may not realize the page has scrolled back to the top unless explicitly informed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');` at the end of the _onClick function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button is assigned to _trigger but the code never sets aria-expanded to reflect the open/closed state of the drawer. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3780: After `_initialised = true;` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` and update the `open` function to call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and the `close` function to call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is given role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox" but aria-expanded is never set to reflect whether the suggestion list is visible. The _open and _close functions in siteSearch do not update this attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4780 (in suggestionListRenderer.mount): After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`. Line ~4810 (in siteSearch._open): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line ~4820 (in siteSearch._close): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle element is assigned role="switch" when it is not a native button, but the code never updates aria-checked to reflect the current state (month vs year). The _syncToggleState function only toggles CSS classes.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~6180 (in pricing.init): After setting role="switch", add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', 'false');` (initial state). Line ~6220 (in _syncToggleState or setPeriod): Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard progressbar missing aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar element has role="progressbar" and aria-valuenow is updated, but aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax are never set. Screen readers cannot calculate the percentage completion without these bounds.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~7880 (in multiStepWizard._updateProgress): Before setting aria-valuenow, add `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0');` and `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100');` (or use the actual step count for max).

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3480 (in srUtils.markInvalid): After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" (or remove the attribute), leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3500 (in srUtils.markValid): After removing aria-describedby references, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for filter results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates and a custom event is emitted, but no aria-live announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the filter has been applied or how many results are showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~5580 (in productFilter.applyFilters, inside the filterAnimation.run callback): After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing missing live region announcement for billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the price values update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~6200 (in pricing.setPeriod, after updating UI): Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcement for open/close state
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~6880 (in faqAccordion.open): After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });` add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);`. Line ~6920 (in faqAccordion.close): After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });` add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);`.

fpTitles: multiStepWizard progressbar missing aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens visually and locks scroll, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true". Screen reader users cannot tell whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1767 (after `_open = true;`): `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never reset to "false", leaving screen reader users with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1791 (after `_open = false;`): `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: On page load, the mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set, violating the requirement that ARIA state attributes reflect the initial closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850 (after `_initialised = true;`): `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not trap focus when open
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens but does not implement a focus trap. Keyboard users can Tab out of the drawer into the background page while the drawer is visually open, creating a focus management failure.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1851 (after setting aria-expanded): `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` and in open() at line 1776 add `_trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true", so screen reader users are not informed that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1917 (after `inst.open = true;`): `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu closes, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never reset to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1931 (after `inst.open = false;`): `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when registered, leaving them without state information until first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2010 (after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`): `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item expands, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true" (the close function sets it to "false", but open never sets it to "true").
issuesFound.fix: Line 2937 (after `item.open = true;`): `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons toggle active state visually via CSS classes, but do not update aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2658 (after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`): `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (Monthly/Annual) update visual active states but do not set aria-pressed to indicate which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2814 (after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`): `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, aria-invalid is not set to "true" on the invalid field; when validation passes, aria-invalid is not cleared. Screen readers cannot convey field validity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3252 (inside else block, after addClass): `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` and Line 3247 (inside if block, after removeClass): `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` (or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`)

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but is not initialised with aria-expanded="false", leaving screen readers unaware of the collapsed state of the suggestion list.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2462 (after mounting listbox): `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters not announcing results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the result count updates visually but is not announced to screen reader users via a live region, leaving them unaware that the product list has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2596 (inside filterAnimation.run callback, after _updateCountBadge): `liveRegion.announce(totalMatches === 0 ? 'No products match your filters' : \`Showing ${totalMatches} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod not announcing billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled, prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen reader users, who may not notice the price change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2803 (before emit): `liveRegion.announce(\`Billing period changed to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}\`);`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles the visibility of the navigation submenu but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the expanded or collapsed state to assistive technology users.
